title: What Is Politics What Is Ideology
description: Glossary / शब्दकोश English Term Nepali Term Nepali Script Meaning Politics Rajniti राजनीति The art and science of governance and power Ideology Vichaardhara विचारधारा A system of ideas and ideals forming the basis of political theory Government Sarkar सरकार The governing body of a state State Rajya राज्य A politically organized community under one government Power Shakti / Satta शक्ति / सत्ता Authority and ability to govern Revolution Kranti क्रान्ति Fundamental overthrow of a political system C

title: Socialism Marxism Communism
description: Chapter 3: Socialism, Marxism & Communism समाजवाद, मार्क्सवाद, साम्यवाद Glossary / शब्दकोश English Nepali Script Meaning Socialism Samajvaad समाजवाद Economic system with collective/state ownership; reduce inequality Communism Saamyavaad साम्यवाद Classless, stateless society; all property owned collectively Marxism Marxvaad मार्क्सवाद Philosophy of Karl Marx: class struggle, historical materialism Leninism Leninvaad लेनिनवाद Lenin’s adaptation: vanguard party leads revolution Maoism Maovaad माओवा

title: Social Democracy
description: Social democracy sits between capitalism and socialism. It accepts:

title: Federalism
description: Federalism (संघीयवाद) is a system where power is divided between a central (federal) government and regional (state/provincial) governments. Each level has its own authority, laws, and institutions. The opposite is a unitary state where all power resides in the central government (like Nepal was before 2015).

title: Theocracy, State Religion and Secularism
description: Theocracy (धर्मतन्त्र) is a system of government where **religious leaders or religious law serve as the supreme authority**. In a true theocracy, the head of state is a religious figure, and laws are derived from sacred texts rather than democratic legislation.

title: Anarchism
description: Anarchism is the belief that all forms of hierarchical government are unnecessary and harmful. Society can organize itself through voluntary cooperation, mutual aid, and direct democracy without the state. Anarchism is NOT chaos — it is a structured vision of society without rulers.

title: Feminism in Politics
description: Feminism (नारीवाद) is the political, social, and economic movement for equality between men and women. It holds that women have been systematically disadvantaged by patriarchal (पितृसत्तात्मक) systems and that political action is needed to achieve equality.

title: Non-Alignment, Geopolitics & Nepal
description: The Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) emerged during the Cold War when the world was divided between the USA-led Western bloc and the USSR-led Eastern bloc. A group of newly independent nations refused to join either side. Founded in 1961 by:

title: Republicanism
description: Republicanism (गणतन्त्रवाद) is the ideology that rejects hereditary rule (monarchy) and holds that the head of state should be elected or appointed, not born into power. A republic is governed by representatives chosen by the people, not by a king. The word comes from Latin res publica meaning "public affair" — the state belongs to the people, not to one family.

title: Populism
description: Populism is a political approach (not a fixed ideology) that frames politics as a struggle between “the pure people” and “the corrupt elite.” Populist leaders claim to speak for the common people against a rigged system. Populism can be left-wing (Hugo Chávez in Venezuela — against economic elites) or right-wing (Donald Trump — against political/cultural elites).
